methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Exowatt is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Hannan Parvizian and Jack Abraham, having raised $140M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable, all operating within the climate and energy sector at similar stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $140M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $280M. The peer-median method examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—producing a median valuation of $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B. The wide spread reflects material heterogeneity within the Series A climate tech cohort, particularly the Watershed outlier.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $149M to $322M, with a median of $228M. This range reflects tension between forward-looking stage-progression metrics and current peer comparables, which pull toward the lower end. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given the $1.6B spread between minimum and maximum comparable, and whether the $140M raised figure warrants upward adjustment independent of stage mechanics. The analysis supports a central estimate near $225M, though any valuation within the $180M–$280M band remains defensible given current data constraints.
